transcript.text: This is Living in Boulder Vlog on the 5th of November of 2013.
This is my first video that I'm doing in bed after moving into my apartment.
I don't know if I'll make it a habit of doing it like this, but you know, it's cool to mix it up some.
I forgot to say in my yesterday's vlog what I spent a whole lot of the time yesterday was raking my mama's leaves.
But anyways, so today I got up, I had insomnia until like 4.
For the last two nights I've had insomnia and I had to be up until 4.
I'm like beat today though, so from the multiple days of sudden exercise.
And I took a half-trazodone, maybe I should have taken a whole-trazodone.
I think I'll start taking whole-trazodones and a melatonin.
So it's, what time is it now? It's like 8, 10 p.m.
I didn't work today. I got up at like 10.
It was, it was like a sunny day, sort of a sunny day, but it was cold as shit.
And I wanted to do something that I hadn't done yet.
So, and you know, I hadn't been on a run yet.
I, you know, I would say I'd been on a road ride because I did that 126-clumber ride up.
And I haven't swum yet.
Um, but yeah, I got up and I think I like did some things that were, I also, and then I, um, met this, um, I had a, you know, cause I got a membership at the gym recently.
I guess they give you like a free hour of a trainer.
So I went in there and I had the free hour with the trainer and it turns out I've been squatting wrong the whole time.
Um, like when I squat, I haven't squatted in like since I started lifting again, but like before when I lifted weights, when I squat, my feet were pointing forward.
But like, you're supposed to put your legs farther apart and like tilt your legs out and then push your feet out as you do it.
And it felt like you, I could do it a lot, a lot better that way.
So yeah, I'm just like, I'm glad you taught me that.
And then the IT band thing, those little foam roller things.
You got me, he showed me some cool stretches to do all that.
And, um, I, um, yeah, I want to start doing that regularly too.
And I, oh, and he showed me these other kinds of squats too, that I want to start doing.
I can't remember what they're called, where you hold the weight in front of you.
Cause I told him, he's like, so what do you do?
And I'm like, oh, well, you know, my sports, the endurance mountain biking.
So that's why he showed me, showed me the squats and stuff.
But then we spent the rest of the time.
Oh shit, where's the piece of paper?
Oh, well, whatever.
Um, cause I had a question about the protein.
I wanted to know how much fat percentage I had.

So, you know, um, I'm at 24% now.
So if I wanted to get down to like 10%, that'd take 14 weeks, which is, uh, three and a half months.
So by the time next spring comes around, I could be 10% body fat.

Bruce Lee.
